message:
perf(raptorq): cap symbol pool initial allocation to per-object demand

RaptorQSender::send_object() previously set the symbol pool's initial_size
to the configured pool size regardless of actual object demand. For small
sends (e.g., 320 symbols needed vs 1024 configured pool), this caused
unnecessary pre-allocation of ~700 unused symbol buffers.

Extracts sender_pool_bounds() to compute deterministic pool sizing:
- initial_size = min(configured_pool_size, source + repair symbols):
  caps the upfront allocation to what the object actually needs, avoiding
  large pre-allocation bursts for small objects
- max_size = max(configured_pool_size, source + repair symbols):
  preserves enough headroom for full source+repair coverage, expanding
  beyond the configured ceiling only when a single object legitimately
  requires it

Adds two regression tests:
- sender_pool_bounds_caps_initial_allocation_to_object_need: verifies
  small-object capping (320 needed, 1024 configured → initial=320, max=1024)
- sender_pool_bounds_preserves_capacity_for_large_objects: verifies
  large-object expansion (1500 needed, 1024 configured → initial=1024,
  max=1500)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
